Articles.Title: Farage's rivals rule out standing in Clacton by-election
Articles.Description: The Reform UK leader says he wants an "establishment versus the people by-election" but Labour calls it a "circus".

Articles.Title: Chris Mason: Farage attempts to seize back the agenda
Articles.Description: His aim throughout the forthcoming campaign is to clearly frame this by-election as "the people versus the establishment".

Articles.Title: US launches strikes on Iran after tankers hit in Strait of Hormuz
Articles.Description: The US says the strikes are intended to impose "heavy costs", while Iran warns it will "take decisive measures" in response.

Articles.Title: Amber heat-health alerts in effect as UK set for one of longest-lasting heatwaves since 1976
Articles.Description: Temperatures are set to rise as high as 36C (97F) for parts of southern England this week.

Articles.Title: 'Extreme' marine heatwave expected for parts of UK
Articles.Description: Scientists fear that high sea temperatures could have wide-ranging consequences for marine life.

Articles.Title: Why replacing leasehold may be easier said than done
Articles.Description: The government wants to abolish leasehold, but replacing it could prove far more complicated

Articles.Title: Egypt fume at 'injustice' and make Messi favouritism claims after exit
Articles.Description: BBC Sport unpicks a game that will go down in World Cup history for featuring a team who were two goals down so late on, but who went on to win without needing extra time.

Articles.Title: Package holidays to Dubai and Egypt cheaper as European prices creep up
Articles.Description: Family deals to many non-European destinations are cheaper this summer than last, as travel nerves have slowed bookings.

Articles.Title: 'It is not normal' - but extraordinary Djokovic defies odds once again
Articles.Description: It is worth remembering that what you are watching is not normal - but Novak Djokovic is anything but ordinary in another thrilling Wimbledon match.

Articles.Title: Watch: Buckling support beams seen inside New York high-rise
Articles.Description: The structure, which is under construction, was evacuated along with surrounding buildings after reports of falling bricks.

Articles.Title: Hundreds of jobs at risk as John Lewis plans to cut some services
Articles.Description: No final decision has been made but the job cuts will happen in the autumn if the redundancy plans are approved.

Articles.Title: Trump looms large as Nato grapples with challenge of rearming Europe
Articles.Description: The US president's inflammatory words have punctuated the image of unity at this crucial gathering, Security Correspondent Frank Gardner writes.

Articles.Title: Tears of relief? Messi adds another incredible chapter to World Cup career
Articles.Description: Argentina great Lionel Messi sheds tears as they fight back to beat Egypt and reach the World Cup quarter-finals.

Articles.Title: England players to be told not to jump hoardings
Articles.Description: England players will be advised not to jump over advertising hoardings after Jordan Henderson suffered a freak injury.

Articles.Title: Fifa investigating alleged racist abuse of IShowSpeed
Articles.Description: Fifa has opened an investigation following allegations of racist abuse involving American social media influencer IShowSpeed and a supporter during Argentina's 3-2 win over Cape Verde on Friday.

Articles.Title: Can Scotland learn from history-making Norway?
Articles.Description: Norway, a nation of a similar size to Scotland, are through to the World Cup quarter-finals, so can lessons be learned from their neighbours from across the North Sea?

Articles.Title: Harry's bad news lands at wrong time, but prince sticks to script
Articles.Description: The Duke of Sussex has returned to the UK, but his first day of engagements was overshadowed by his defeat in court.

Articles.Title: Watch: Clacton's mixed views on Farage's resignation
Articles.Description: Nigel Farage announced he is resigning as an MP, triggering a by-election he will stand in.

Articles.Title: The Papers: 'Let the people judge' and 'Farage gamble turns to farce'
Articles.Description: Nigel Farage's announcement he was resigning as an MP to trigger a by-election is the lead of many of Wednesday's papers.

Articles.Title: Presidency bid while continuing court fight a gamble for Le Pen
Articles.Description: Hard-right leader Marine Le Pen is appealing her case to France's top civil court, but it could backfire.

Articles.Title: Alan Carr is keeping one unwanted item from his castle auction – see what was sold
Articles.Description: A concrete cow was the only one of the hundreds of items from Ayton Castle that failed to find a buyer.

Articles.Title: Reform asks NCA to investigate leaks of private financial information
Articles.Description: The party's deputy leader Richard Tice says he found out payments to his company had been flagged to the agency, after questions from the media.

Articles.Title: Harry loses High Court privacy case against Daily Mail publisher
Articles.Description: Judge Mr Justice Nicklin says the claimants had failed to prove the allegations.

Articles.Title: Hungary's public news broadcasts halted in bid to scrap Orban-era propaganda
Articles.Description: The country's main state TV channel displayed a message saying it was "sorry" for lying on Tuesday.

Articles.Title: Lawyer says detained Gaza doctor was severely beaten in Israeli jail
Articles.Description: The lawyer for Dr Hussam Abu Safiya tells the BBC his client was so badly beaten he could not recognise him during a visit last week.

Articles.Title: One million more UK homeowners set  to face higher mortgages
Articles.Description: Homeowners face paying £45 a month more on average when they move onto a new deal over the next two years.

Articles.Title: Online gamblers betting more than £1,000 to face new checks
Articles.Description: Punters who bet more than £1,000 online in a 24-hour window will have to undergo an assessment, the regulator says.

Articles.Title: Russia could compete at LA 2028 Olympics
Articles.Description: Russian athletes could be allowed to compete for their country at the 2028 Olympic Games in Los Angeles after the International Olympic Committee provisionally lifted their suspension.

Articles.Description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

Articles.Title: Nigel Farage Resigns As MP
Articles.Description: Nigel Farage resigns as MP but says he’ll fight the by-election.

Articles.Description: Historian Heather Cox Richardson on what history tells us about modern America

Articles.Title: Superb England inflict record T20 defeat on India
Articles.Description: England inflict a record defeat on India in the third Twenty20 at Trent Bridge to give the home side a 2-0 lead with two matches in the series remaining.

Articles.Title: Fulham appoint Arbeloa as Silva's successor
Articles.Description: Fulham appoint Alvaro Arbeloa as their new manager on a three-year contract, succeeding Marco Silva who left for Benfica.

Articles.Title: 'How?!' - Gauff battles into first Wimbledon semi-final
Articles.Description: Coco Gauff surprised even herself by overcoming her recent grass-court struggles to reach a first Wimbledon semi-final against Karolina Muchova.

Articles.Title: Final extension: Startup Battlefield Australia applications now close July 20
Articles.Description: If you're building something ambitious, this is a fast track to the people who can move your startup forward.

Articles.Title: Meta just launched a new AI generator, Muse Image, and users are already pushing back over use of their photos
Articles.Description: The new image-generating model has numerous use cases, including advertising, decorating and creator-based opportunities.

Articles.Title: Why the rise of open source AI isn’t hurting Anthropic … yet
Articles.Description: Open source models’ success isn’t coming at the expense of frontier labs. Instead, they each seem to capture two phases of the same life cycle.

Articles.Title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
Articles.Description: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.

Articles.Title: Discord admits AI moderation bug wrongfully banned users over harmless images
Articles.Description: The company confirmed that the issue had been affecting accounts since May, with an additional 200 users banned over the weekend before its team identified and fixed the problem.

Articles.Title: Google’s Pixel event is set for August 12
Articles.Description: Google's upcoming event in August will introduce new Pixel devices.

Articles.Title: Figma acquires team behind a vibe-coding app
Articles.Description: The Y Combinator-backed company started a vibe-coding platform and later built an agent-creation product.

Articles.Title: Netflix dabbles in shorter video content with its new set of publisher deals with Variety, others
Articles.Description: Netflix is bringing 2- to 20-minute videos to its platform through new partnerships with digital publishers, including Rolling Stone and Variety.

Articles.Title: Hacked, leaked, and held for ransom: The worst breaches of 2026 so far
Articles.Description: From a massive DOGE data breach and the hacking of critical energy and water systems to the hack of an FBI surveillance system, here are the most damaging security incidents and data breaches of 2026.

Articles.Title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
Articles.Description: With this update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.

Articles.Title: X adds a video editor to encourage creators to post original content, not stolen reposts
Articles.Description: X is rolling out a new video editor and recorder for iOS with multilingual captions, green-screen effects, and other editing tools.

Articles.Title: VC firm Chemistry is raising $500M for its second fund
Articles.Description: Chemistry Ventures, the VC firm launched by Bessemer, Index Ventures, and Andreessen Horowitz alums, is raising $500M for its second fund.

Articles.Title: AI law startup Norm raises $120M, hits unicorn valuation
Articles.Description: AI law startup Norm has raised a $120 million Series C round led by Khosla Ventures, valuing the startup at $1.2 billion.

Articles.Title: This startup pits dealerships against each other to bid on your used car
Articles.Description: Bidbus, which lets dealerships bid on used cars, has raised $15 million in a Series A round that was led by early-stage mobility fund Ibex Investors.

Articles.Title: Hacktivists call out Trump by hacking and defacing US Army websites
Articles.Description: The U.S. Army has fixed two of its websites that were hacked to display messages calling President Trump a "pedophile" and a "thief."

Articles.Title: Savi’s app aims to protect consumers from realistic AI scams like kidnappers demanding ransom
Articles.Description: The company just raised $7 million in seed funding, and is launching its app for iPhone and Android on Tuesday.

Articles.Title: The first American autonomous ground vehicles are fighting in Ukraine
Articles.Description: Forterra has deployed more than 100 of its self-driving ATVs in conflict zones in Ukraine.

Articles.Title: Netflix invented binge-watching. Now it may have outgrown it.
Articles.Description: A new report suggests Netflix viewers aren’t sticking around for Season 2. The bigger issue may be that binge-watching itself is no longer the advantage it once was.

Articles.Title: The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
Articles.Description: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied stolen credentials — meaning it wasn't quite the fully autonomous cybercrime debut that last week's headlines suggested.

Articles.Title: US investors will soon get access to SK Hynix, another memory maker riding the AI boom
Articles.Description: SK Hynix is experiencing a boom credited to AI. It will ride that to a multibillion-dollar U.S. IPO, expected to take place on Friday.
